prompt: How do I make pasta better?
completion: Salt the water like the sea, finish the pasta in the sauce, and save a ladle of starchy water to emulsify.
A one-pan roast chicken with lemon and thyme, crisp skin, and pan juices spooned over everything.
A splash of acid at the end — lemon or vinegar — wakes up almost any dish.
Sheet-pan salmon with olive oil, garlic, and seasonal vegetables roasted until caramelized.
Dry the surface, season generously, sear hot for a deep crust, then rest it before slicing across the grain.
Soft scrambled eggs over buttered sourdough with blistered cherry tomatoes and good coffee.

prompt: How do I make soup richer?
completion: Build a base of sweated aromatics, deglaze, and simmer with a parmesan rind for savory depth.
A simple panna cotta: warm cream, bloom gelatin, sweeten lightly, and chill until just set.
